name: Saarland (Germany)
description: The German tricolour of black, red, and gold horizontal stripes, with the quartered Saarland coat of arms placed at the centre.
history: Adopted on 9 July 1956, on the eve of the Saar Protectorate's political reintegration with West Germany on 1 January 1957. The flag combines the post-war federal tricolour with a quartered shield drawn from the four historic territories that make up the modern state.

features.symbolism: The German national tricolour, asserting the Saarland's identity as a German state after its return from French administration in 1957.

features.role: Saarland coat of arms (a quartered shield: the silver-and-blue lion of Nassau-Saarbrucken, the red Trier cross on silver, the gold-and-red lion of Pfalz-Zweibrucken on blue, and the silver alerions on red of the Duchy of Lorraine)
features.symbolism: Each quarter recalls one of the four historical territories whose lands together make up the modern Saarland: the County of Saarbrucken, the Electorate of Trier, the Palatinate-Zweibrucken, and the Duchy of Lorraine.
